# Effortless AWS

> Code-first AWS Lambda framework for TypeScript. Export handlers, deploy with one command.

Effortless AWS is a TypeScript framework where you define AWS resources (Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS, S3, SES, CloudFront, Fargate, MCP) by exporting handler objects from `.ts` files. The framework handles all infrastructure — IAM roles, Lambda Function URLs, event source mappings, Lambda layers — automatically. No YAML, no CloudFormation, no state files.

- Package: `effortless-aws` (npm)
- CLI: `eff deploy`
- Docs: https://effortless-aws.website

## Project setup

```typescript
// effortless.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "effortless-aws";

export default defineConfig({
  name: "my-service",
  region: "eu-central-1",
  handlers: ["src/**/*.ts"],
  stage: "dev",
  lambda: {
    memory: 256,
    timeout: "30 seconds",
    runtime: "nodejs24.x",
  },
});
```

All Lambdas run on ARM64 (Graviton2). Not configurable.

## Core concepts

### Handler = exported const

Every `export const x = define*({...})` creates AWS resources. The export name becomes the resource name.

### deps = automatic IAM wiring

Reference other handlers via `.deps(() => ({ orders, uploads, mailer }))`. The framework injects typed clients and creates IAM permissions automatically.

### config = SSM secrets

Use `defineSecret()` inside the `.config()` builder to declare SSM secrets:

```typescript
.config(({ defineSecret }) => ({
  apiKey: defineSecret(),                          // string, key inferred from property name
  port: defineSecret({ transform: Number }),       // number
  config: defineSecret({ transform: JSON.parse }), // parsed JSON
  token: defineSecret({ generate: "hex:32" }),     // auto-generated on first deploy
  id: defineSecret({ generate: "uuid" }),          // auto-generated UUID
}))
```

SSM path: `/${project}/${stage}/${key}`. The legacy `param()` function is deprecated — use `defineSecret()` instead.

### setup = cold-start factory

`.setup(async ({ deps, config }) => ({ db: createPool(config.dbUrl) }))` runs once per cold start. Return value properties are **spread directly into callback args** (no `ctx` wrapper). `deps`, `config`, and `files` are available in `.setup()` only — not in callbacks. Pass Lambda options as a second arg: `.setup(fn, { memory: 512 })`, or alone: `.setup({ memory: 512 })`.

### schema = validation

Pass a Standard Schema V1 compliant validator (Zod, ArkType, Valibot, etc.) for runtime validation, or use the generic parameter on `define*<T>()` for type-only. Validated data arrives as `data` arg in callbacks.

### static = bundled files

Use `.include("src/templates/*.ejs")` builder method to bundle files into Lambda ZIP. Access via `files.read("src/templates/invoice.ejs")` in setup.

### Shared builder methods

All handler types support the following builder chain: `.deps()`, `.config()`, `.include()`, `.setup()`, `.onError()`, `.onCleanup()`. Examples below show these only where relevant — they work the same everywhere.

## Handler types

### defineApi — HTTP API

Creates Lambda + Function URL. Routes via chained `.get()`, `.post()`, `.put()`, `.delete()`, `.patch()` with a route definition object. Builder chain: `.deps()` → `.config()` → `.include()` → `.auth()` → `.setup()` → `.onError()` → `.onCleanup()` → route methods.

```typescript
import { defineApi, defineTable } from "effortless-aws";
import { z } from "zod";

type Session = { userId: string; role: string };
export const users = defineTable<{ tag: string; name: string }>().build();

export default defineApi({ basePath: "/api" })
  .deps(() => ({ users }))
  .config(({ defineSecret }) => ({ dbUrl: defineSecret() }))
  .auth(async ({ config }) => ({
    secret: config.dbUrl,
    expiresIn: "7d",
    apiToken: {
      header: "x-api-key",
      verify: async (token) => ({ userId: token, role: "api" }),
      cacheTtl: "5m",
    },
  }))
  .setup(async ({ deps, config }) => {
    const pool = createPool(config.dbUrl);
    return { pool };
  })
  .get({ path: "/users" }, async ({ pool }) => ({
    status: 200,
    body: await pool.query("SELECT * FROM users"),
  }))
  .get({ path: "/users", cache: "30s" }, async ({ pool }) => ({
    status: 200,
    body: await pool.query("SELECT * FROM users"),
  }))
  .post({ path: "/users", input: z.object({ name: z.string() }) }, async ({ input, pool }) => ({
    status: 201,
    body: { id: crypto.randomUUID() },
  }))
  .post({ path: "/login", public: true }, async ({ input, auth }) => {
    return auth.createSession({ userId: "admin", role: "admin" });
  });
```

**Route definition object:** `{ path: "/...", public?: boolean, input?: StandardSchemaV1, cache?: CacheOptions }`. Routes are authenticated by default when `.auth()` is configured. Set `public: true` to skip auth. Unmatched routes → 404, missing session → 401.

**Cache options (GET routes):** `cache: "30s"` (shorthand) or `cache: { ttl: "30s", swr: "5m", scope: "public" | "private" }`. Shorthand defaults to public scope with `swr = ttl * 2`.

**Stream mode:** `defineApi({ basePath: "/api", stream: true })` enables Server-Sent Events. Route handlers receive a `stream: ResponseStream` argument.

**`.auth()` builder method** — enables typed session auth. Auth helpers spread into route args:
- `auth.createSession(data, options?)` — set signed cookie, returns `{ status, headers }`
- `auth.clearSession()` — clear cookie
- `auth.session` — current session (`T | undefined`)
- Supports hybrid API token auth via `apiToken: { verify: fn, cacheTtl: "5m", header: "x-api-key" }`

**Route callback args:** `req`, `input` (validated), `ok`, `fail`, `auth` (if configured), `stream` (if stream mode), plus all properties from `setup` return.

**`ok` / `fail` helpers:** `ok(body?, status?)` returns success response. `fail(message, status?)` returns error response.

**`req` object:** `req.method`, `req.path`, `req.headers`, `req.query`, `req.body` (auto-parsed JSON), `req.rawBody`.

### defineTable — DynamoDB table

Creates a DynamoDB table with fixed envelope: `{ pk, sk, tag, data: T, ttl? }`. Optionally creates a stream Lambda via `onRecord`/`onRecordBatch`. Builder chain: `.deps()` → `.config()` → `.include()` → `.setup()` → `.onError()` → `.onCleanup()` → `.onRecord()` / `.onRecordBatch()` / `.build()`.

```typescript
import { defineTable } from "effortless-aws";

type Order = { tag: string; amount: number; status: string };

// Resource-only
export const orders = defineTable<Order>().build();

// With custom tag field name
export const orders = defineTable<Order>({ tagField: "type" }).build();

// With stream handler (builder chain)
export const orders = defineTable<Order>()
  .deps(() => ({ customers }))
  .stream({ batchSize: 100, concurrency: 5 })
  .setup(({ deps }) => ({ customers: deps.customers }))
  .onRecord(async ({ record, customers }) => {
    // record.eventName: "INSERT" | "MODIFY" | "REMOVE"
    // record.new?.data / record.old?.data — typed as Order
    // record.keys — { pk, sk }
  });
// OR: .onRecordBatch (return { failures: string[] } for partial batch failure)
```

**Table options:** `billingMode`, `tagField` (custom discriminant field name, default "tag"), `schema` (Standard Schema validator).
**Stream options** (via `.stream({...})`): `streamView`, `batchSize`, `batchWindow`, `startingPosition`, `concurrency` (parallel processing, default 1), `maxRetries`.

**TableClient API** (via `deps`):

```typescript
await table.put({ pk: "USER#1", sk: "PROFILE", data: { tag: "user", name: "Alice" } });
await table.put({ pk: "...", sk: "...", data: {...}, ttl: ts }, { ifNotExists: true });
const item = await table.get({ pk: "USER#1", sk: "PROFILE" }); // item.data — typed
await table.delete({ pk: "USER#1", sk: "PROFILE" });
await table.query({ pk: "USER#1", sk: { begins_with: "ORDER#" }, limit: 10, scanIndexForward: false });
await table.queryByTag({ tag: "user", limit: 50 }); // GSI
await table.update({ pk: "USER#1", sk: "ORDER#1" }, {
  set: { status: "shipped" }, append: { events: ["shipped"] }, remove: ["tempNotes"],
  increment: { count: 1 }, tag: "shipped-order", ttl: ts,
});
```

**SK conditions:** `string` (exact match), `{ begins_with }`, `{ gt }`, `{ gte }`, `{ lt }`, `{ lte }`, `{ between: [a, b] }`.

### defineQueue — SQS queue (FIFO or standard)

Creates SQS queue + Lambda consumer. Builder chain: `.poller()` → `.deps()` → `.config()` → `.include()` → `.setup()` → `.onError()` → `.onCleanup()` → `.onMessage()` / `.onMessageBatch()`.

```typescript
import { defineQueue } from "effortless-aws";

type OrderEvent = { orderId: string; action: "created" | "shipped" };

export const orderQueue = defineQueue<OrderEvent>({
  fifo: true,
  visibilityTimeout: "120s",
  contentBasedDeduplication: true,
})
  .poller({ batchSize: 10, batchWindow: "10s" })
  .onMessage(async ({ message }) => {
    // message.body — typed as OrderEvent
    // message.messageId, message.messageGroupId
  });
// OR: .onMessageBatch (return { failures: string[] } for partial batch failure)
```

**Queue options:** `fifo` (enable FIFO semantics), `visibilityTimeout`, `retentionPeriod`, `delay`, `contentBasedDeduplication`, `maxReceiveCount`.

**Poller options** (via `.poller({ ... })`): `batchSize` (1-10), `batchWindow` (Duration — `"5s"`, `"1m"`, etc.).

**Sending** (via `deps`):

```typescript
await orderQueue.send({ body: { orderId: "1", action: "created" }, groupId: "order-1" });
await orderQueue.sendBatch([
  { body: { orderId: "1", action: "created" }, groupId: "order-1" },
  { body: { orderId: "2", action: "shipped" }, groupId: "order-2" },
]);
```

**Send options:** `{ body: T, groupId: string, deduplicationId?: string, messageAttributes?: Record<string, { dataType: string; stringValue: string }> }`.

### defineBucket — S3 bucket

Creates S3 bucket. Optionally creates event Lambda. Supports typed entity storage. Builder chain: `.deps()` → `.config()` → `.include()` → `.entity()` → `.setup()` → `.onError()` → `.onCleanup()` → `.onObjectCreated()` / `.onObjectRemoved()` / `.build()`.

```typescript
import { defineBucket } from "effortless-aws";

// Resource-only
export const uploads = defineBucket({}).build();

// With entities (typed JSON storage)
export const storage = defineBucket({})
  .entity<"users", { name: string; email: string }>("users")
  .entity<"settings", { theme: string }>("settings", { cache: "1h" })
  .build();

// With event handler
export const images = defineBucket({ prefix: "uploads/", suffix: ".jpg" })
  .onObjectCreated(async ({ event, bucket }) => {
    // event.key, event.size, event.eTag, event.eventTime
    const file = await bucket.get(event.key);
  });
```

**Bucket options:** `prefix`, `suffix` (event filtering), `seed` (local directory — uploaded once on first deploy), `sync` (local directory — synced on every deploy, deletes removed files).

**BucketClient API** (via `deps`): `put(key, body, opts?)`, `get(key)`, `delete(key)`, `list(prefix?)`, `bucketName`.

**StoreEntityClient API** (via `deps` when entities are defined): `put(id, data)`, `get(id)`, `delete(id)`, `list()`. Each entity is accessible as a property: `storage.users.put("1", { name: "Alice", email: "a@b.com" })`.

### defineCron — Scheduled Lambda

Creates EventBridge Scheduler + Lambda. Builder chain: `.deps()` → `.config()` → `.include()` → `.setup()` → `.onError()` → `.onCleanup()` → `.onTick()` (terminal).

```typescript
import { defineCron } from "effortless-aws";

export const cleanup = defineCron({ schedule: "rate(2 hours)" })
  .onTick(async () => { /* ... */ });

export const sync = defineCron({ schedule: "cron(0 9 * * ? *)", timezone: "Europe/Moscow" })
  .deps(() => ({ orders }))
  .config(({ defineSecret }) => ({ apiKey: defineSecret() }))
  .setup(async ({ deps }) => ({ db: deps.orders }))
  .onTick(async ({ db }) => { /* ... */ });
```

**Schedule:** `"rate(5 minutes)"`, `"rate(1 hour)"`, `"rate(1 day)"` | `"cron(0 9 * * ? *)"` (6 fields: min hour dom month dow year). Supports `timezone` option with full IANA timezone autocomplete (418 zones).

### defineMailer — SES email

Creates SES email identity with DKIM. Resource-only, used via `deps`.

```typescript
// defineMailer is curried — call defineMailer() to get the config function
export const mailer = defineMailer()({ domain: "myapp.com" });
// Via deps: await mailer.send({ from, to, subject, html?, text? })
```

### defineWorker — Long-running Fargate worker

Creates SQS queue + Fargate container. The worker auto-scales: starts when messages arrive, shuts down after idle timeout. Builder chain: `.deps()` → `.config()` → `.include()` → `.setup()` → `.onError()` → `.onCleanup()` → `.onMessage()` (terminal).

```typescript
import { defineWorker } from "effortless-aws";

type Job = { videoId: string; format: string };

export const encoder = defineWorker<Job>({
  size: "2vCPU-4gb",
  idleTimeout: "10m",
  concurrency: 3,
})
  .deps(() => ({ uploads }))
  .setup(async ({ deps }) => ({ bucket: deps.uploads }))
  .onMessage(async (msg, { bucket }) => {
    // msg — typed as Job
    // long-running processing here
  });
```

**Worker options:** `size` (Fargate size: `"0.25vCPU-512mb"`, `"0.5vCPU-1gb"`, `"1vCPU-2gb"`, `"2vCPU-4gb"`, `"4vCPU-8gb"`, default `"0.5vCPU-1gb"`), `idleTimeout` (default `"5m"`), `concurrency` (max parallel messages, 1-10, default 1).

**onMessage callback:** receives `(msg: T, ctx)` where `msg` is the typed message body and `ctx` contains setup return properties.

**onError callback:** receives `{ error, msg, retryCount }`. Return `"delete"` to discard the message, `"retry"` (default) to reprocess.

**WorkerClient API** (via `deps`):

```typescript
await encoder.send({ videoId: "abc", format: "mp4" });
await encoder.send({ videoId: "abc", format: "mp4" }, { delay: "5m", start: false });
const status = await encoder.status(); // "running" | "idle"
await encoder.stop();
```

### defineMcp — Model Context Protocol server

Creates Lambda + Function URL implementing the MCP protocol. Exposes tools, resources, and prompts to MCP clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.). Builder chain: `.deps()` → `.config()` → `.include()` → `.auth()` → `.setup()` → `.onError()` → `.onCleanup()` → `.tool()` / `.resource()` / `.prompt()` / `.build()`.

```typescript
import { defineMcp, defineTable } from "effortless-aws";
import { z } from "zod";

export const db = defineTable<{ tag: string; title: string; content: string }>().build();

export default defineMcp({ name: "my-tools", version: "1.0.0", instructions: "A helpful assistant" })
  .deps(() => ({ db }))
  .setup(async ({ deps }) => ({ db: deps.db }))
  .tool(
    { name: "search", description: "Search notes", input: z.object({ query: z.string() }) },
    async (input, { db }) => {
      const results = await db.queryByTag({ tag: "note", limit: 10 });
      return results.map(r => r.data.title);
    }
  )
  .tool(
    { name: "create", description: "Create a note", input: { type: "object", properties: { title: { type: "string" }, content: { type: "string" } }, required: ["title", "content"] } },
    async (input, { db }) => {
      await db.put({ pk: "NOTE#" + crypto.randomUUID(), sk: "META", data: { tag: "note", ...input } });
      return "Created";
    }
  )
  .resource(
    { uri: "resource://notes/{id}", name: "Note", params: z.object({ id: z.string() }) },
    async ({ id }, { db }) => {
      const note = await db.get({ pk: "NOTE#" + id, sk: "META" });
      return note?.data;
    }
  )
  .prompt(
    { name: "summarize", description: "Summarize notes", args: [{ name: "topic", required: true }] },
    async ({ topic }) => `Please summarize all notes about: ${topic}`
  );
```

**MCP options:** `name` (server name), `version` (default `"1.0.0"`), `instructions` (system prompt context sent to clients).

**`.tool(def, handler)`** — `def: { name, description, input }`. Input can be a Standard JSON Schema V1 (e.g. Zod schema) for typed inference, or a raw `{ type: "object", properties, required? }` object. Handler receives `(input, ctx)`. Return value is auto-wrapped as text content; return `{ content: McpToolContent[], isError? }` for explicit control.

**`.resource(def, handler)`** — `def: { uri, name, params?, description?, mimeType? }`. URI can be a template with `{param}` placeholders. Handler receives `(params, ctx)`. Return string or `McpResourceContent[]`.

**`.prompt(def, handler)`** — `def: { name, description?, args? }`. Args can be Standard Schema V1 or `McpPromptArgument[]`. Handler receives `(args, ctx)`. Return a string (auto-wrapped as user message) or `McpPromptResult` with explicit messages.

**Auth:** `.auth()` works identically to `defineApi` — uses signed cookies with optional API token support.

### defineApp — SSR framework deployment

Creates CloudFront + Lambda Function URL + S3 for SSR frameworks.

```typescript
// defineApp is curried — call defineApp() to get the config function
export const frontend = defineApp()({
  server: ".output/server",         // directory with index.mjs handler
  assets: ".output/public",         // static assets → S3
  build: "nuxt build",
  path: "/",                        // base URL path (default: "/")
  domain: "app.example.com",        // optional (needs ACM cert in us-east-1)
  lambda: { memory: 1024 },         // Lambda settings live under `lambda`
  routes: { "/api/*": apiHandler }, // proxy API routes via CloudFront
});
// Stage-specific: domain: { prod: "app.example.com", staging: "staging.example.com" }
```

Supports: Nuxt (native), Next.js (via OpenNext), any Nitro-based framework, custom `handler(event, context)`.

### defineStaticSite — static site / SPA on CloudFront

Creates S3 + CloudFront distribution. Builder chain: `.route()` → `.middleware()` → `.build()`.

```typescript
import { defineStaticSite } from "effortless-aws";

export const docs = defineStaticSite({
  dir: "dist",
  build: "npm run build",
  domain: "docs.example.com",
  errorPage: "404.html",
  seo: {
    sitemap: "sitemap.xml",
    googleIndexing: "path/to/service-account-key.json",
  },
})
  .route("/api/*", apiHandler, { access: "private" })
  .middleware(async (request) => {
    // request.uri, request.method, request.querystring, request.headers, request.cookies
    if (!request.cookies.session) return { redirect: "/login" };
    // return { status: 403, body?: "..." } to deny
    // return void to allow
  })
  .build();
```

**Config options:** `dir`, `index` (default `"index.html"`), `build`, `errorPage`, `domain` (string or stage-keyed record), `seo: { sitemap, googleIndexing? }`.

**`.route(pattern, handler, opts?)`** — proxy routes to another handler (e.g., API) via CloudFront. Eliminates CORS. `opts: { access?: "private" | "public" }`.

**`.middleware(fn)`** — Lambda@Edge function for request-level auth/redirects. Return `{ redirect: "/path", status? }` to redirect, `{ status: 403 }` to deny, or `void` to allow.

## CLI commands

```bash
eff deploy                         # deploy all handlers
eff deploy ./src/api.ts            # deploy specific file
eff deploy createUser              # deploy specific handler
eff deploy --stage prod            # deploy to production
eff status                         # compare code vs deployed
eff logs processOrder --tail       # stream CloudWatch logs
eff logs processOrder --since 1h   # logs from last hour
eff config                         # interactive SSM setup
eff config list                    # show all params
eff config set stripe/secret-key   # set specific parameter
eff cleanup                        # preview orphaned resources
eff cleanup --all                  # delete all resources
eff layer                          # show dependency layer info
eff layer --build                  # build layer locally
```

All commands accept `--project`, `--stage`, `--region`, `--verbose`.

### MCP server

The CLI ships as an MCP server (`eff-mcp`) that can be connected to Claude Desktop, Cursor, or any MCP client. It exposes tools for deploying, viewing logs, managing config, checking status, and cleaning up resources — all within the AI assistant context.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "effortless-aws": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["eff-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Key patterns and conventions

### Naming

All AWS resources follow `${project}-${stage}-${handlerName}`: Lambda `my-service-dev-createUser`, DynamoDB `my-service-dev-orders`, SQS `my-service-dev-orderQueue.fifo`.

### Stage isolation

Each stage gets fully independent resources. No shared infrastructure between `dev` and `prod`.

### No state files

Resources are discovered via AWS resource tags: `effortless-project`, `effortless-stage`, `effortless-handler`, `effortless-component`.

### Lambda layer

Production `dependencies` from `package.json` → shared Lambda Layer. AWS SDK v3 excluded (provided by runtime). Hash-versioned, re-uploaded only when deps change.

### Duration format

Durations can be a number (seconds) or string: `"30s"`, `"5m"`, `"1h"`, `"1d"`. Helper: `toSeconds(duration)`.

### Callback args

Handler callbacks receive a single object (or positional args for `defineWorker`). Args are conditionally present based on configuration:
- `req` — HTTP request (defineApi only)
- `input` — validated input (when schema/input is set on route)
- `ok`, `fail` — response helpers (defineApi only)
- `auth` — session helpers (when `.auth()` is configured)
- `stream` — SSE stream (when `stream: true` on defineApi)
- `...ctx` — setup return properties spread directly into args
- `record` / `records` / `message` / `messages` / `event` / `bucket` — event data (type-specific)

`deps`, `config`, `files` are available in `setup` only — not in callbacks.

### config with defineSecret

```typescript
.config(({ defineSecret }) => ({
  apiKey: defineSecret(),                                  // string
  port: defineSecret({ transform: Number }),               // number
  config: defineSecret({ transform: JSON.parse }),         // parsed JSON
  secret: defineSecret({ generate: "hex:32" }),            // auto-generate on first deploy
  id: defineSecret({ generate: "uuid" }),                  // auto-generate UUID
}))
```

The legacy `param("key")` and `param("key", transform)` still work but are deprecated.
